name: Cofrentes Volcanic Trail
name_original: Volcán de Agrás — circular desde Cofrentes
description: Hike the moderate 6.4-mile circular route around the extinct Agrás Volcano from Cofrentes, enjoying unique geology and river views.
region_intro: Hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos traverse a landscape characterized by the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, which have carved deep gorges and valleys. The region features a mix of Mediterranean forests, agricultural lands, and rugged terrain, with the Embalse de Embarcaderos reservoir as a central feature. Elevations vary, offering both riverside paths and routes with moderate climbs through hills and wooded areas. The area provides diverse hiking opportunities within the El Valle de Cofrentes-Ayora natural park. Best hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos The most popular hiking route is The Castle of Chirel (PR-CV 382), a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete, leading to historical ruins with panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Campichuelo Trail, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Local hikers also love the La Noria, Casas de Alcance, Cofrentes y el Río Cabriel — circular desde Hervideros de Cofrentes, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and past small villages, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Hiking in Embalse De Embarcaderos is defined by river gorges, reservoir views, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes with elevation gain. The routes in Embalse De Embarcaderos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 760 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse De Embarcaderos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Embalse De Embarcaderos offers a diverse network of over 50 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging paths with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Yes, the region has 17 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These routes often follow the riverbanks or offer gentle ascents through the Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Absolutely. Many of the easier trails are suitable for families. Look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances, which allow everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check specific regulations for the El Valle de Cofrentes-Ayora natural park or individual routes, particularly concerning protected areas or wildlife.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The best times to hike in Embalse De Embarcaderos are spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable for hiking.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130614671/la-noria-casas-de-alcance-cofrentes-y-el-rio-cabriel-circular-desde-hervideros-de-cofrentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Noria, Casas de Alcance, Cofrentes y el Río Cabriel — circular desde Hervideros de Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a varied loop through river valleys and past villages.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The diverse habitats of Embalse De Embarcaderos, including river gorges and Mediterranean forests, support a variety of wildlife. You might spot birds of prey, various waterfowl near the reservoir and rivers, and possibly small mammals. The region is part of a natural park, emphasizing its ecological value.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos offer views of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, the Embalse de Embarcaderos reservoir, and Mediterranean forests. You can also discover historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt; or natural wonders such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4963542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 6 difficult routes available. These trails typically feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094673631/alto-de-alcola-circular-desde-cofrentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Alcola — circular desde Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbing.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning river gorges, the panoramic views from elevated points like those on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e817941873/the-castle-of-chirel-pr-cv-382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle of Chirel (PR-CV 382)&lt;/a&gt;, and the peaceful immersion in nature.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points for hikes, especially near villages like Cofrentes or recreational areas, offer designated parking spaces. It's advisable to check specific route details or local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: While the trails themselves are often in natural settings, you can find cafes and restaurants in nearby towns and villages such as Cofrentes. These establishments offer opportunities to refuel before or after your hike.
tips: A beautiful bridge from where you can see the Cabriel River and the Embarcaderos reservoir.
In this corner of the Ayora Valley there is a jewel that makes it very special. It is the dormant volcano of Agrás, whose spring waters are used in the Hervideros spa.
It is somewhat hidden but it is a beautiful place. On the banks of the Cabriel River and under a spectacular tree, it is a place worth visiting to rest or take photos.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate hike. You'll encounter varied terrain, including reddish, gypsum-rich soil near the river and distinctive volcanic rock like basalt and lapilli closer to the volcano. Expect some pronounced and potentially slippery slopes, especially as you approach the summit.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Volcán de Agrás circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll cross the Cofrentes Bridge, explore the unique Agrás Volcano, and enjoy a fantastic View of Cofrentes Castle and the Cabriel River. Informative panels along the way explain the local geology and volcanism.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead in Cofrentes?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in Cofrentes. While specific parking details aren't provided, Cofrentes is the main access point, and you should be able to find parking within the town or near the start of the route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with varied terrain or near wildlife. It's advisable to keep them on a leash.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months. Winters are generally mild.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Volcán de Agrás circular route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What makes the Agrás Volcano geologically significant?
faqs.answer: The Cerro de Agrás is unique as the only recent volcanic outcrop in the Valencian Community, an extinct Pliocene-Quaternary stratovolcano active less than two million years ago. You can still observe remnants like a large volcanic bomb, lava flows, solidified basalt rocks, and volcanic chimneys within the crater. Its magmatic chamber still feeds the thermal springs of the nearby Hervideros spa.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other official trails. You'll find it overlaps with parts of El Campichuelo, Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance, La Chirrichana, Los Cañones del Júcar, and Camino del Balneario.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated moderate, the trail does have some pronounced and potentially slippery slopes. For families with younger children or absolute beginners, it might present a challenge. However, the interpretive panels and unique volcanic features can make it an engaging experience for those comfortable with moderate hikes.

faqs.question: Are there any local tips for experiencing the volcano?
faqs.answer: Beyond hiking, there's a local tourist train that can take visitors up to the volcano, offering an alternative way to experience the unique geological site. Also, while much of the ascent can be done via a road, there's an option to climb cross-country for a more adventurous experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Cofrentes?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cofrentes is a town with amenities. You'll find various options for cafeterias, restaurants, and accommodation within the municipality, making it convenient for pre- or post-hike refreshments and stays.

waypoints.name: View of Cofrentes Castle and the Cabriel River
